metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_866
metadata.comment:
Rationale: By tissue fractionation of mouse whole brain [Fig2] or human post mortem hippocampus [Fig3], DTNBP1 (Dysbindin-1) is found concentrated in the synaptosome and synaptic vesicle fractions, with little protein in the PSD fraction. Immuno-EM (qualitative) confirmed presynaptic vesicle localisation in the mouse and macaque dentate gyrus inner molecular layer [Fig5] and CA1 [Fig8A & B], and the dentate gyrus hilus of the mouse [Fig6C]. Postsynaptic density localisation was also detected in the dentate gyrus hilus [Fig6B & D] mossy fiber synapse [Fig7] and in the CA1 area of mouse [Fig8D & E].. Experimental description: Mouse, macaque and human material is used, as specified in figure legends:
Fig2: mouse, Fig3 human, Fig5A macaque Fig5B & C mouse, Fig6 mouse, Fig 7 mouse, Fig8A macaque, Fig8B-E mouse. All endogenous protein.

19/6/2017 Pim
- A wealth of species is used in this paper. I have selected *mouse* as annotated species because biochem data and EM data are both available for this species.
